response.justification: The candidate is an experienced full-stack/web engineer with strong craftsmanship credentials, but they fail the role's hard requirements: no Japanese proficiency (native-level required; they list English, Romanian, and German), no Linux kernel investigation or modification experience, and no background in L2/L3 networking, switch products, or security technologies like MACsec or 802.1X. Their C experience is limited to one early-career role on a C IDE product, far from the deep embedded C/UNIX proficiency the position demands.
strengths: Some C/C++ background (NI LabWindows/CVI components, C++ audio thesis on Linux)
Broad engineering experience including low-latency systems and hardware-adjacent work (robotic arms, embedded-ish tooling)
Strong testing/TDD discipline and Linux, Docker, CI/CD familiarity
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency — an explicit native-level requirement
No Linux kernel, networking (L2/L3, switch), or security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X) experience
Career trajectory centers on web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node, D), not embedded firmware development

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking/security or Linux kernel experience
